The video opens with a close-up of a man's hands holding a large, pale orange isopod-like crustacean, showcasing its segmented body and antennae against a white background. Sonny Side Water Expert, wearing an orange life vest with '長兼丸' and a 7-Eleven headband, stands on a fishing boat in a harbor, holding the same crustacean and excitedly explaining, 'This is the isopod. It's got this little tail, kinda similar to a lobster. And then its mouth, what is its mouth even doing?' He describes it as 'soft and it's full of food,' noting that 'every animal that dies and just drifts to the bottom of the ocean, these guys munch on that.' He humorously adds, 'It's honestly like God told Satan, you can design one creature, he made this and then he goes, "okay, great, but that can only be on the bottom of the ocean."' A squishy sound accompanies his description of the creature's texture. The scene transitions to the crustacean crawling on a burlap surface next to dark, shell-like objects, followed by a wider shot of the white fishing boat docked at a harbor with buildings and a 'Marine Agency ITOH SHOJI KAISHA, LTD.' sign. The video cuts to yellow buoys and ropes on the boat, then to Mr. Hasegawa, a deep-sea fisherman in a pink apron and plaid shirt, working on the deck. A wide aerial view of a large cargo ship in the harbor is shown before returning to the fishing boat, where a woman in an orange life vest uses a phone as the boat moves through choppy waters. Sonny Side Water Expert is seen bracing himself on the rocking deck, expressing discomfort, narrating over a "Yoi Yoi" song with French lyrics, 'Chizzy is out. The producer is out. The camera guys are out. They tussled with the ocean and lost. I found a deckhand to hold the camera, and Chizzy's loss has become Captain Hasegawa's gain, as he joins me for a seaside continental brunch.' Mr. Hasegawa and Sonny Side Water Expert then sit on the deck, with Mr. Hasegawa handling a black net and sorting small, dark crustaceans on a burlap cloth, while Sonny Side watches. Sonny Side encourages, 'Let's jump into it. Can you show me how to do it?' as Mr. Hasegawa demonstrates, 'he takes the head, he kind of hollows that out, then he cuts that away.' The final shot is a close-up of Mr. Hasegawa's hands as he carefully handles one of the small crustaceans, emphasizing the detailed work involved in the fishing process, accompanied by background music.

The video opens with a close-up of weathered hands cracking open a dark, segmented crustacean, revealing its orange, fibrous interior. On a teal fishing boat, Mr. Hasegawa, in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, sits beside Sonny Side Water Expert, who wears a blue shirt, orange life vest, and a 7-Eleven headband, at a burlap-covered table with several similar crustaceans. Sonny, holding a cracked crustacean, reacts with wide-eyed surprise, questioning, "We can eat that?" Mr. Hasegawa calmly offers, "Dōzo," prompting Sonny to humorously ask, "Wait, only me?" and then, "But what about both of us?" Mr. Hasegawa explains, "Omotenashi, dōzo," as Sonny, with a laugh, admits he doesn't understand the words but gets the message: he's the one who paid for the trip and never wanted to eat 'isopods.' Despite his humorous reluctance, Sonny declares, "Okay, I'm gonna eat this one. I'll do it," and proceeds to break open another crustacean, revealing its white, flaky meat. A distinct crunching sound is heard as he takes a bite, exclaiming "Oishii!" and describing the taste as "sweet," with a "little bit of exhaust fumes and charcoal," and a "crabby kind of flavor." He then decides to eat another, explaining his rule about eating two if he can eat one, and notes that Japanese TV crews usually stir-fry these, but he prefers Mr. Hasegawa's "chimney smoking method," finding it "kind of romantic." The camera zooms in on the meat's texture before pulling back to show Sonny's continued enthusiastic eating, while Mr. Hasegawa watches with a calm, amused expression. The background reveals a harbor under an overcast sky, and the scene briefly cuts to a pile of dark crustaceans before showing Mr. Hasegawa at the bow of the boat, gripping a metal pole as the vessel moves through choppy waters with sparkling light effects, all set to a gentle, slightly melancholic instrumental music fading in.

event_blocks.summary: A traditional Japanese melody accompanies the cracking open of a large, dark, segmented crustacean aboard a teal fishing boat, revealing its orange and white interior as Mr. Hasegawa, a deep-sea fisherman in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, demonstrates the preparation to Sonny Side Water Expert, who wears a blue shirt, orange life vest with '長兼丸' printed vertically, and a 7-Eleven headband. Sonny inquired, "Are you gonna eat with me?", and after Mr. Hasegawa responded with "Taberu?" (Eat?), Sonny affirmed, "Yes," and proposed, "Cheers." Mr. Hasegawa exclaimed, "Saiko!" (The best!), which Sonny echoed, humorously adding, "You gotta be a psycho to eat this. But actually, saiko means excellent in Japanese." Sonny then asked about "shark extract" to please his girlfriend, eliciting laughter and another "Saiko!" from Mr. Hasegawa, leading Sonny to declare, "I'm a psycho!" The scene shifted to the dock, where they stood by a white flatbed truck; Sonny, holding a small jar of liquid, exclaimed, "Oh my God, shark oil," and asked, "Should I drink it or what do I do?" After Mr. Hasegawa's encouragement to "Drink!", Sonny sipped, remarking, "It's like fish oil," to which Mr. Hasegawa responded with a surprised "Eh?" Sonny then humorously warned against demonetization before expressing, "Incredible experience. Thank you so much. You killed it. Arigato gozaimasu," and Mr. Hasegawa replied, "Domo." The video concluded with a wide shot of the teal fishing boat, registration 'S02-3205' and '静岡県1352', navigating choppy waters, accompanied by Sonny's excited "Woo!" and the bold yellow text 'COME ON! WHA!'.

Mr. Hasegawa.description: A deep-sea fisherman in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, demonstrating and explaining the preparation of a dark crustacean with calm, deliberate gestures. He speaks Japanese, saying 'Taberu?' (Eat?), exclaiming 'Saiko!' (The best/Psycho!) often with a laughing tone, making a surprised 'Eh?' sound, and concluding with 'Domo' (Thanks).

Teal Fishing Boat.description: A teal and white fishing boat with registration 'S02-3205' and '静岡県1352', shown moving through choppy waters, equipped with fishing gear, ropes, and a winch, with crew members actively managing the vessel. The sounds of choppy waters are present as it moves.

White Flatbed Truck.description: A white flatbed truck parked at the dock, serving as a backdrop for the interaction between Mr. Hasegawa and Sonny Side Water Expert, with visible Japanese text on its side.

Sonny Side Water Expert.description: A man in a blue shirt, orange life vest with '長兼丸' printed vertically, and a white headband with a 7-Eleven logo, actively tasting the crustacean, reacting with animated expressions, and later sipping from a small jar of liquid on the dock. He speaks English and some Japanese, asking 'Are you gonna eat with me?', exclaiming 'Psycho!' and 'Oh my God, shark oil', questioning 'Should I drink it or what do I do?', and later expressing 'Incredible experience. Thank you so much. You killed it. Arigato gozaimasu.' He also makes an excited 'Woo!' sound, often with a humorous and energetic tone.

Mr. Hasegawa, a deep-sea fisherman in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, demonstrates the preparation to Sonny Side Water Expert, who wears a blue shirt, orange life vest with '長兼丸' printed vertically, and a 7-Eleven headband. Sonny then asks about "shark extract" to make his girlfriend happy, prompting a laughing "Saiko!" from Mr. Hasegawa and Sonny declaring, "I'm a psycho!" event_blocks.summary: Mr. Hasegawa, a deep-sea fisherman, pulls a thick rope on a teal and white fishing boat amidst choppy waters, as Sonny Side Water Expert narrates about learning from isopods and the challenges of perseverance, even when the ocean fights against you and the crew might not speak to you again. The crew pulls in black cylindrical fishing cages, tracked by a 'CAGE COUNTER', while a yellow buoy floats nearby. The boat navigates rough seas with mountains in the background. A large isopod-like crustacean is cracked open, revealing its fibrous interior, and Mr. Hasegawa sorts small, dark crustaceans. After a brief shot of a white flatbed truck, the scene shifts to a serene aerial view of a rural Japanese landscape with golden rice fields and mountains, featuring a butterfly and a bee on rice stalks. Sonny and Shizuka welcome viewers to their Japan series, focusing on Kobe, the beef capital, where black cows are sprayed with water on a traditional Japanese farm. The narrative then transitions to a bustling Japanese street scene with a 'KOBE BEEF STEAK' sign and a seafood banner, highlighting the abundance of beef restaurants. At a rural farm, the Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top observe black cows under an overcast sky. Golden, marbled beef sears on a grill, and a large 'KOBE BEEF' sign is displayed. The voiceover discusses the high auction prices for cattle, justifying the cost of Kobe beef due to its quality and genetics. A tour of a modern barn shows black cows being fed from automated troughs, and the Man with Headband, Woman in White Tank Top, and a Farm Worker in a Blue Shirt interact. The segment promises a look behind the scenes of a Kobe beef farm and a live auction to understand how Japan engineered the best beef. In a refrigerated meat processing facility, Meat Processing Workers inspect large cuts of marbled beef, and in a meat auction room, Auction Workers examine and score beef carcasses amidst rapid chanting. The scene shifts to an urban setting with 'KOBE KUYORYUCHI' and 'BEAMS HOUSE' signage, leading to 'STEAK HOUSE en KOBE BEEF' with a stylized cow logo. At the restaurant, upbeat hip-hop music plays, and two female voices exclaim "Itadakimasu!" followed by a male voice welcoming viewers to "N Kobe." The video then enters a high-end Teppanyaki restaurant, described as a "Kobe Beef live performance." The Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top are seated as the narrator explains Teppanyaki. Chef Mr. Kita Takahiro meticulously slices marbled beef. The chef prepares vegetables and serves an appetizer of Kobe beef tongue with various accompaniments. The narrator highlights that they will experience "the finest beef in the world, Kobe Wagyu," noting the high cost and sourcing from local breeders. The couple enjoys dishes like braised meat and marbled beef, with a golden plaque confirming 'Hyogo Prefecture Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef.' The segment concludes with the chef preparing beef and the Man with Headband driving a golf cart, as the narrator states they will see how the beef got there.

Mr. Hasegawa.description: A deep-sea fisherman in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, actively pulling a rope and demonstrating the preparation of a dark crustacean with calm, deliberate gestures.

Bee in Rice Field.description: A bee with a black and yellow body perched on a rice stalk, emphasizing the ecosystem of the rice fields.

Tokyo Street Scene.description: A bustling urban street in Tokyo, with pedestrians walking past a 7-Eleven store, under bright daylight, featuring a blue bus and green trees.

White Flatbed Truck.description: A white flatbed truck parked at the dock, serving as a backdrop for the interaction between Mr. Hasegawa and Sonny Side Water Expert, with visible Japanese text on its side.

Map of Japan.description: An animated map showing a route from Shizuoka to Kanagawa, with a red dot marking Kanagawa and a blue line tracing the journey, overlaid on a blue background.

Butterfly in Rice Field.description: A butterfly with black and white wings resting on golden rice stalks, highlighting the natural beauty and biodiversity.

Crustacean.description: A large, pale orange isopod-like crustacean with a segmented body, multiple legs, and long antennae, shown being held and cracked open to reveal its orange and white fibrous interior, served on a burlap cloth.

Small Dark Crustaceans.description: Small, dark-colored crustaceans, possibly isopods or similar, shown being sorted on a burlap cloth by Mr. Hasegawa, with close-ups highlighting their texture and movement.

Teal Fishing Boat.description: A teal and white fishing boat with registration 'S02-3205' and '静岡県1352', shown moving through choppy waters, equipped with fishing gear, ropes, and a winch, with crew members actively managing the vessel.

Golden Field.description: A vast, sunlit field of golden crops with mountains in the background, shown in an aerial view, introducing the rural setting of Kitakata, Fukushima.

Yellow Buoy.description: A bright yellow, cylindrical buoy floating in the choppy sea, bobbing with the waves, visible in a wide shot of the ocean.

Traditional Japanese Farm.description: A covered barn with black cows, being sprayed with water, and a man in a blue shirt and white headband inspecting the cows, with a woman in a white tank top standing beside him.

On-Screen Text Overlay.description: Bold, yellow, 3D-style text appearing on screen, including 'OMOSHIROI!!!', 'YOI YOI', 'TIP!', and a 'CAGE COUNTER' that increments from 2 to 6, used to emphasize actions and count retrieved fishing cages.

Black Cylindrical Cages.description: Several black, cylindrical fishing cages with perforated sides, shown stacked on the boat, being handled by the crew, and emptied into a black bucket, with an on-screen 'CAGE COUNTER' tracking their retrieval from 2 to 6.

Sonny Side Water Expert.description: A man in a blue shirt and orange life vest with '長兼丸' printed vertically, actively tasting the crustacean with an animated expression and later sipping from a small jar of liquid on the dock. He narrates about 'Isopods' teaching him lessons, expresses a desire to 'puke' from the experience, and later introduces himself as 'Sonny' for a 'Japan series' segment, discussing Kobe as 'Japan's beef capital' with the 'highest value, most coveted beef around the world'.

Marbled Beef Carcass.description: A large, hanging cut of beef with intricate white marbling, labeled with a tag, displayed in a refrigerated meat processing facility, shown as the male voiceover announces a 'rare look behind the scenes' of Kobe beef production.

Meat Processing Worker.description: A worker in a white coat, hairnet, and cap, handling large cuts of marbled beef in a refrigerated facility, inspecting and moving the carcasses with care, seen as the male voiceover describes getting a 'rare look behind the scenes' of Kobe beef production.

Auction Worker.description: A worker in a white coat and cap, walking through a meat auction room, inspecting hanging beef carcasses while other workers in white uniforms sit at desks, scoring the meat, all set against the backdrop of rapid, rhythmic auctioneer-like chanting as the male voiceover announces joining a 'live auction'.

Automated Feeding Troughs.description: Rows of metal feeding troughs in a barn, equipped with yellow automated feed dispensers, where black cows are eating hay, seen as the male voiceover discusses the quality and genetics of Kobe beef.

Meat Auction Room.description: A large, well-lit room with rows of hanging beef carcasses, workers in white uniforms seated at desks with scorecards, and a central aisle where a worker inspects the meat, serving as the setting for a 'live auction' with rapid, rhythmic auctioneer-like chanting.

Man with Headband.description: A man wearing a white headband with a 7-Eleven logo and a gray shirt, seated at a restaurant counter, eating with chopsticks, reacting with excitement, exclaiming 'Wow, such an appetizer' and 'This is amazing. It's like it's dinner and a show at the same time,' and later seen driving a golf cart.

Black Cows in Pasture.description: A herd of black cows grazing in a grassy field, enclosed by a wooden fence, under an overcast sky, with a barn and trees in the background, observed by the Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top as the male voiceover expresses curiosity about Kobe beef's price.

Woman in White Tank Top.description: A woman in a white tank top, seated beside the Man with Headband at the restaurant counter, eating with chopsticks, smiling, and reacting with enjoyment to the food, noting 'Whoa, jumps around a lot' during cooking.

City Street Signage.description: A vertical brown sign with 'KOBE KUYORYUCHI' in white text, mounted on a building, and a modern building with 'BEAMS HOUSE' in large white letters, indicating an urban commercial area, marking the transition from rural tranquility to urban sophistication as the male voiceover describes revealing the secrets of Kobe beef.

Farm Worker in Blue Shirt.description: A man in a blue shirt and glasses, standing inside a barn, gesturing toward the cows while speaking to the Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top, as the male voiceover discusses the high price and quality of Kobe beef.

Restaurant Sign.description: A white sign with brown text reading 'STEAK HOUSE en KOBE BEEF' and 'LIVE PERFORMANCE', featuring a stylized cow logo and Japanese characters, mounted above the restaurant entrance.

Searing Pan.description: A dark, non-stick pan used to sear a piece of marbled beef, showing visible sizzling and browning as the meat is cooked with salt and pepper.

Chef in White Uniform.description: A chef wearing a white uniform, tall white hat, and red apron, identified as 'veteran Teppanyaki chef and owner, Mr. Kita Takahiro,' skillfully slicing marbled beef with a large knife, demonstrating precision and care in a professional kitchen setting, and preparing an appetizer of 'Kobe beef tongue, orange, mint, horseradish, and green and purple onion.'

Marbled Beef.description: A large, high-grade cut of beef with intricate fat marbling, specifically identified as 'Kobe Wagyu,' 'the finest beef in the world,' with a large steak costing 'about $250,' and sourced from 'local breeders.' It is shown being sliced and presented on a white plate with green garnishes, emphasizing its premium quality.

Restaurant Interior.description: A modern, warm-toned restaurant with wooden slat walls, long counter tables, ambient lighting, and large windows, creating an elegant and sophisticated dining atmosphere.

Braised Meat Dish.description: A rich, dark, saucy braised meat dish served in a red bowl, garnished with green onions and a slice of orange, being eaten with chopsticks by the Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top.

Golf Cart.description: An orange and black golf cart with a transparent canopy, driven by the Man with Headband, suggesting a transition to a different location or activity.

Golden Plaque.description: A golden plaque with Japanese text '兵庫県但馬牛 神戸肉' (Hyogo Prefecture Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef), displayed on a wooden stand, signifying the restaurant’s specialty in authentic Kobe Beef.

raw_segments: The segment opens with an upbeat, driving musical score as Mr. Hasegawa, a deep-sea fisherman in a purple plaid shirt, pink apron, and light green headband, vigorously pulls a thick rope on a teal and white fishing boat, marked 'S02-3205' and '静岡県1352', which rocks violently in choppy waters. Sonny Side Water Expert narrates, stating, "Isopods taught me more than I could ever learn about them," as a close-up reveals a pile of cooked, orange-colored crustaceans, possibly mantis shrimp, with the bold yellow text 'OMOSHIROI!!!' overlaid. Sonny continues, "They taught me that even if your goal is hard, I'm almost definitely gonna puke today. Look forward to that," as he animatedly tastes a crustacean, later sipping from a small jar on the dock. The narration emphasizes perseverance, "Even if the ocean is fighting against you," coinciding with the crew pulling in black cylindrical fishing cages, tracked by an on-screen 'CAGE COUNTER' from 2 to 6, and a yellow buoy floating in the rough sea. A wide shot shows the boat navigating waves with mountains in the background, as Sonny adds, "Even if your crew will never speak to you again... Sometimes you need to push past the pain in pursuit of something bigger." The scene transitions to a large, pale orange isopod-like crustacean being cracked open to reveal its fibrous interior on a burlap cloth, where Mr. Hasegawa also sorts small, dark crustaceans. After a brief cut to a white flatbed truck at the dock, the music shifts to a serene tone, accompanying an aerial view of a rural Japanese landscape with golden rice fields and distant mountains, followed by close-ups of a butterfly and a bee on rice stalks. The music then builds again as Sonny introduces, "Welcome back to our Japan series. My name is Sonny," joined by a female voice saying, "And I'm Shizuka." Sonny announces, "And today we're in Kobe," adding, "It makes you wanna vomit, doesn't it, right?" The scene shows a traditional Japanese farm with black cows being sprayed with water, and a man inspecting them with a woman beside him, as Sonny narrates, "Japan has the highest value, most coveted beef around the world. And Kobe is Japan's beef capital." The segment concludes with a bustling urban street scene in Japan, featuring a 7-Eleven, and a map of Japan overlaid, indicating a journey from Shizuoka to Kanagawa.

The video opens with a bustling Japanese street scene, featuring a restaurant with a prominent 'KOBE BEEF STEAK' sign, colorful balloons, and a seafood banner, as a male voiceover notes the abundance of beef restaurants around the main station, stating he's "obsessed with beef." Upbeat, slightly mysterious instrumental music begins as the scene transitions to a rural farm where the Man with Headband and the Woman in White Tank Top observe black cows in a pasture under an overcast sky. A close-up shows golden, marbled beef searing on a grill, followed by a large 'KOBE BEEF' sign. The male voiceover explains it's "not unusual to see cattle selling at auction for $10 to $15,000 a head," expressing curiosity about how to "justify that price" for Kobe beef, despite its known quality and genetics. Visually, an aerial view of a sprawling farm complex among green hills leads to a modern barn where black cows are fed from automated troughs. The Man with Headband and the Woman in White Tank Top interact with a Farm Worker in a Blue Shirt, who gestures toward the cows. The voiceover announces a "rare look behind the scenes," touring a Kobe beef farm and joining a live auction to reveal "how Japan has engineered the best beef in the world." The scene shifts to a refrigerated meat processing facility where Meat Processing Workers inspect large cuts of marbled beef hanging from hooks, with a close-up revealing intricate marbling and a label. The video then cuts to a meat auction room, where Auction Workers examine and score beef, and another worker inspects hanging carcasses, accompanied by rapid, rhythmic auctioneer-like chanting. The chanting continues as the atmosphere shifts to urban sophistication with a city street featuring 'KOBE KUYORYUCHI' and 'BEAMS HOUSE' signage, culminating at a restaurant named 'STEAK HOUSE en KOBE BEEF' with a 'LIVE PERFORMANCE' sign and a stylized cow logo, all set to the swelling, upbeat instrumental music. The video concludes at the 'STEAK HOUSE en KOBE BEEF' restaurant as upbeat hip-hop music with male vocals plays, urging to "turn big dreams into action" and "mix action with the passion." Two female voices then exclaim "Itadakimasu!" (a Japanese phrase said before eating), followed by a male voice saying "Thank you," welcoming viewers to "N Kobe."

The video opens in a warm, wooden high-end Teppanyaki restaurant, introduced by an upbeat, jazzy soundtrack as a "Kobe Beef live performance" that "lives up to its name." The Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top walk along a long counter, then are seated as the narrator explains Teppanyaki is "flat-top Japanese cooking, right before your eyes with your very own personal chef." The Chef in White Uniform, identified as "veteran Teppanyaki chef and owner, Mr. Kita Takahiro," meticulously slices a large piece of marbled beef. The camera pans across the modern dining area before focusing on the chef preparing food. The Man with Headband exclaims, "Wow, such an appetizer," as the chef gets "some vegetables going," accompanied by sizzling sounds. The Woman in White Tank Top reacts, "Whoa, jumps around a lot," and the Man with Headband adds, "This is amazing. It's like it's dinner and a show at the same time." The chef serves an appetizer of "Kobe beef tongue, orange, mint, horseradish, and green and purple onion." The narrator highlights that they will experience "the finest beef in the world, Kobe Wagyu," noting a large steak costs "about $250" and the restaurant only sources from "local breeders." The couple enjoys their meal with chopsticks, savoring dishes like braised meat and beautifully plated marbled beef. A close-up of a golden plaque labeled '兵庫県但馬牛 神戸肉' (Hyogo Prefecture Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef) reinforces the restaurant’s specialty. The segment concludes with the chef continuing to prepare the beef, and the Man with Headband driving a golf cart, accompanied by golf cart sounds, as the narrator states, "before we dive into that marbled chunk of heaven, we're going to see exactly how it got here."

event_blocks.summary: Under an overcast sky, upbeat instrumental music plays as a Man with Headband, wearing a 7-Eleven branded headband, drives an orange golf cart across a rural farm's dirt path, passing a large white silo and farm buildings. He dismounts and walks towards a covered barn, where he meets a Farm Worker in Blue Shirt, identified by a narrator as Mr. Nitoshi, whose farm has been passed down for 80 years. The narrator explains they are there to uncover the secrets of Japanese beef, specifically Kobe, and to confirm or dispel rumors about farmers massaging cows, playing them classical music, or feeding them beer. The Man with Headband, the Farm Worker, and a Woman in White Tank Top, who shares a "cute laugh" and promises to keep secrets, inspect black cows in the well-ventilated barn, which features automated feeding troughs and wooden stalls. The Farm Worker gestures toward the cows, some with large horns and yellow ear tags, including a close-up of one labeled '4436', while the Man with Headband listens intently and occasionally points. An aerial view showcases the entire farm complex with its multiple buildings, green fields, and a small pond. A split-screen montage highlights different cow breeds (Shorthorn, Polled, Brown, Black) as the narrator identifies the observed breed as the Black Tajima, one of Japan's four famous domestic cattle breeds, found in Hyogo Prefecture. The Man with Headband asks if a cow has a name, and the Woman in White Tank Top replies that Mr. Nitoshi calls him "Kuro," meaning black. The Farm Worker then gently touches a cow's head and neck, demonstrating his care and familiarity in the calm atmosphere, as the discussion continues about livestock management and breed characteristics. The segment opens with a close-up of a black cow, its head secured by a purple rope halter, displaying a yellow ear tag labeled '49067', as a hand gently strokes its back. A male voice bids farewell to a cow, stating, "Well, 8443, it's been nice getting to know you." The scene transitions to a wider view of a well-lit, rustic barn where multiple black cows with ear tags and some with nose rings calmly feed from metal troughs. A narrator explains that farmers, like 'Nitoshi', are uncertain of their cattle's meat rating or auction value until the sale, thus they prioritize optimal living conditions, including fresh mountain air, reasonable space, and high-quality food. The Farm Worker in Blue Shirt is seen hand-feeding hay into the troughs, an action further elaborated by a woman's voice (Woman in White Tank Top) who explains that he provides hay and water, sourcing the highest quality hay from Australia, Canada, and the US. The narrator then adds that the cows also receive a mixture of corn, soy scraps, and other greens three times a day, noting that corn constitutes a much larger portion of cattle's diet in the US compared to Kobe. The Man with Headband, wearing a 7-Eleven headband, walks through the barn, observing the cows and occasionally touching them, then asks a female voice (Woman in White Tank Top), "If someone were to buy a cow, how much would that cost?" She responds, "One cow would cost about a million yen." He follows up, "How do you justify that price?" to which she explains that the breed is decreasing in numbers, leading to higher demand. The sequence concludes with a close-up of a cow’s face, its yellow ear tag clearly visible, as it chews hay, emphasizing the individual care given to each animal amidst the ongoing discussion about their value and care. The video opens inside a rustic, open-sided barn with wooden beams and corrugated metal roofing, where several black cows with yellow ear tags are feeding from long metal troughs filled with hay, their distinct mooing sounds occasionally heard. A narrator explains that only about 3,000 cattle qualify as Kobe quality each year, with farmers like Nitoshi prioritizing quality over quantity. The Man with Headband, wearing a gray shirt and a 7-Eleven headband, walks through the barn alongside the Farm Worker in Blue Shirt, who feeds the cows by hand. The Woman in White Tank Top joins them, observing with interest. As the camera pans to a water sprinkler system spraying mist over the barn floor, the Man with Headband asks about "water misting," to which the Woman in White Tank Top clarifies, "Sometimes it's water, but actually it's a sanitization liquid," explaining it's "to keep them clean" and that they get "showered" about "once every five minutes." The group moves to a different section where cows are tethered with ropes and nose rings; the Man with Headband gently touches a cow’s back while the Farm Worker in Blue Shirt holds its rope. The Man with Headband expresses curiosity about the "mythical lore about Kobe beef," questioning "what's real and what's bullshit" and calling some claims "bullshit." The Woman in White Tank Top laughs and gestures animatedly, debunking the "massage rumor" as "probably false," suggesting it might only happen "if a TV company came." The Man with Headband then jokingly asks if they could "pretend to massage them for our video." He then asks if they "play classical music," to which the Woman in White Tank Top responds that the farmer "actually tried doing that at the beginning, playing his own music that he liked a little bit, but then he just stopped." The scene includes a wide shot of the barn’s interior with hay bales and automated feeding systems, all under natural, diffused sunlight, creating a warm, rural atmosphere as the group learns about the farm's operations, accompanied by upbeat background music.

Woman in White Tank Top.description: A woman in a white tank top, standing beside the Man with Headband in the barn, observing the cows, listening to the discussion with interest, and gesturing as she speaks, laughing at one point. She is heard speaking, clarifying that the mist is a sanitization liquid, explaining the frequency of cow 'showers,' and debunking the massage rumor, often with a clear, informative, and sometimes amused tone, also laughing.

Farm Worker in Blue Shirt.description: A man in a light blue work shirt and glasses, feeding the cows by hand, placing hay into metal troughs, holding a cow’s rope, and explaining details to the Man with Headband and Woman in White Tank Top, though his specific voice is not clearly identifiable as a primary speaker in this segment.

Black Cows.description: Large black cows with yellow ear tags, some with prominent horns and nose rings, housed in a covered barn with wooden stalls and automated feeding troughs, appearing calm and well-cared-for, feeding on hay. Their distinct mooing sounds are heard, particularly a prominent 'moo' sound effect.

Automated Feeding Troughs.description: Rows of metal feeding troughs in the barn, equipped with yellow automated feed dispensers, where black cows are seen eating hay, indicating modern farm technology.

Golf Cart.description: An orange and black golf cart with a transparent canopy, driven by the Man with Headband across a dirt path on a farm, featuring large black tires and a visible 'ZM40' model label on the front.

Split-Screen Cow Breeds.description: A four-panel split-screen montage showing different cow breeds labeled 'Shorthorn', 'Polled', 'Brown', and 'Black', each with a distinct color and physical trait, used to visually compare breeds during the discussion.

Yellow Ear Tag 4436.description: A close-up of a yellow ear tag on a black cow, clearly labeled '4436', used for identification and tracking within the farm's livestock management system.

Aerial View of Farm.description: An expansive aerial shot of a rural farm complex, showing multiple buildings, green fields, a small pond, and surrounding trees, captured under an overcast sky, providing context for the setting.

Man with Headband.description: A man wearing a white headband with a 7-Eleven logo and a gray shirt, walking through the barn, observing the cows, and engaging in conversation with the Farm Worker in Blue Shirt and the Woman in White Tank Top, occasionally touching the cows. He is heard speaking frequently, asking questions about the farm's operations, the misting system, and the myths surrounding Kobe beef, including whether cows are massaged or listen to classical music, often with an inquisitive and slightly skeptical tone, and laughing.

The segment opens with a close-up of a black cow, its head secured by a purple rope halter, displaying a yellow ear tag labeled '49067', as a hand gently strokes its back. A male voice bids farewell to a cow, stating, "Well, 8443, it's been nice getting to know you." The scene transitions to a wider view of a well-lit, rustic barn where multiple black cows with ear tags and some with nose rings calmly feed from metal troughs. A narrator explains that farmers, like 'Nitoshi', are uncertain of their cattle's meat rating or auction value until the sale, thus they prioritize optimal living conditions, including fresh mountain air, reasonable space, and high-quality food. The Farm Worker in Blue Shirt is seen hand-feeding hay into the troughs, an action further elaborated by a woman's voice (Woman in White Tank Top) who explains that he provides hay and water, sourcing the highest quality hay from Australia, Canada, and the US. The narrator then adds that the cows also receive a mixture of corn, soy scraps, and other greens three times a day, noting that corn constitutes a much larger portion of cattle's diet in the US compared to Kobe. The Man with Headband, wearing a 7-Eleven headband, walks through the barn, observing the cows and occasionally touching them, then asks a female voice (Woman in White Tank Top), "If someone were to buy a cow, how much would that cost?" She responds, "One cow would cost about a million yen." He follows up, "How do you justify that price?" to which she explains that the breed is decreasing in numbers, leading to higher demand. The sequence concludes with a close-up of a cow’s face, its yellow ear tag clearly visible, as it chews hay, emphasizing the individual care given to each animal amidst the ongoing discussion about their value and care.

The video opens inside a rustic, open-sided barn with wooden beams and corrugated metal roofing, where several black cows with yellow ear tags are feeding from long metal troughs filled with hay, their distinct mooing sounds occasionally heard. A narrator explains that only about 3,000 cattle qualify as Kobe quality each year, with farmers like Nitoshi prioritizing quality over quantity. The Man with Headband, wearing a gray shirt and a 7-Eleven headband, walks through the barn alongside the Farm Worker in Blue Shirt, who feeds the cows by hand. The Woman in White Tank Top joins them, observing with interest. As the camera pans to a water sprinkler system spraying mist over the barn floor, the Man with Headband asks about "water misting," to which the Woman in White Tank Top clarifies, "Sometimes it's water, but actually it's a sanitization liquid," explaining it's "to keep them clean" and that they get "showered" about "once every five minutes." The group moves to a different section where cows are tethered with ropes and nose rings; the Man with Headband gently touches a cow’s back while the Farm Worker in Blue Shirt holds its rope. The Man with Headband expresses curiosity about the "mythical lore about Kobe beef," questioning "what's real and what's bullshit" and calling some claims "bullshit." The Woman in White Tank Top laughs and gestures animatedly, debunking the "massage rumor" as "probably false," suggesting it might only happen "if a TV company came." The Man with Headband then jokingly asks if they could "pretend to massage them for our video." He then asks if they "play classical music," to which the Woman in White Tank Top responds that the farmer "actually tried doing that at the beginning, playing his own music that he liked a little bit, but then he just stopped." The scene includes a wide shot of the barn’s interior with hay bales and automated feeding systems, all under natural, diffused sunlight, creating a warm, rural atmosphere as the group learns about the farm's operations, accompanied by upbeat background music.

event_blocks.summary: Inside a Japanese farm barn, the Man with Headband and the Woman in White Tank Top observed black cows with yellow ear tags being sprayed with water and fed hay from automated troughs, prompting the Woman in White Tank Top to exclaim "Green Day" and the Man with Headband to express disbelief. A narrator speculated the cows might be "getting them buzzed on Japanese beer," to which the Woman in White Tank Top playfully responded, "He drinks the beer," followed by laughter. The narrator explained that Japan's beef cattle possess superior genetics, meticulously bred for hundreds of years, and the Woman in White Tank Top added they are "trying to protect the DNA," known for a "particularly pure bloodline," with the narrator concluding this "prized DNA" would never leave the country except as steak. The scene shifted to an aerial view of the farm, then to a high-end restaurant with warm wooden interiors where the couple, seated, heard clinking glasses as the Man with Headband suggested a "cheers" to a "special moment," with the Woman in White Tank Top adding "Kanpai!" She hummed in enjoyment, and the Man with Headband declared, "I love Sake," before suggesting "one more" dish, "Kobe on a leaf." After another clink, the Woman in White Tank Top hummed again, and the Man with Headband found it "very yummy," agreeing with her "Mhm." He described it as "nothing too crazy, adding a little bit of temptation," while she called it "the perfect appetizer," stating they are "ready for the real deal next." Upbeat music began as the camera focused on beautifully plated marbled beef, with a narrator announcing they were "moments from biting into the ultimate A5 Wagyu, beef that is world-renowned." The chef in a white uniform precisely sliced a large piece of marbled beef as the narrator expressed eagerness, "I want nothing more than to get this started," and curiosity, "I gotta know what A5 even means." The final shot highlighted the beef's intricate marbling, accompanied by a golden plaque confirming its authenticity as '兵庫県但馬牛 神戸肉' (Hyogo Prefecture Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef).

Woman in White Tank Top.description: A woman in a white tank top, standing beside the Man with Headband in the barn, observing the cows, listening to the discussion with interest, and gesturing as she speaks. She is heard exclaiming "Green Day," playfully responding "He drinks the beer" with laughter, stating they are "trying to protect the DNA" and noting their "pure bloodline." Later, she joins in a "cheers" with "Kanpai!", hums in enjoyment, agrees with "Mhm," calls a dish "the perfect appetizer," and states they are "ready for the real deal next."

Chef in White Uniform.description: A chef wearing a white uniform, tall white hat, and red apron, skillfully slicing marbled beef with a large knife, demonstrating precision and care in a professional kitchen setting.

Marbled Beef.description: A large, high-grade cut of beef with intricate fat marbling, shown being sliced and presented on a white plate with green garnishes, emphasizing its premium quality. It is described by a narrator as "the ultimate A5 Wagyu, beef that is world-renowned," with curiosity expressed about what "A5 even means."

Restaurant Interior.description: A modern, warm-toned restaurant with wooden slat walls, long counter tables, ambient lighting, and large windows, creating an elegant and sophisticated dining atmosphere. Sounds of clinking glasses and conversation are heard within this setting.

Black Cows.description: Large black cows with yellow ear tags, some with prominent horns and nose rings, housed in a covered barn with wooden stalls and automated feeding troughs, appearing calm and well-cared-for, feeding on hay. Their genetics and "pure bloodline" are discussed by the speakers and a narrator.

Automated Feeding Troughs.description: Rows of metal feeding troughs in the barn, equipped with yellow automated feed dispensers, where black cows are seen eating hay, indicating modern farm technology.

Aerial View of Farm.description: An expansive aerial shot of a rural farm complex, showing multiple buildings, green fields, a small pond, and surrounding trees, captured under an overcast sky, providing context for the setting.

Man with Headband.description: A man wearing a white headband with a 7-Eleven logo and a gray shirt, seen walking through the barn, observing the cows, and engaging in conversation with the Woman in White Tank Top, occasionally touching the cows. He is heard expressing disbelief, laughing, suggesting a "cheers" for a "special moment," declaring "I love Sake," introducing a dish as "Kobe on a leaf," and finding it "very yummy."

Golden Plaque.description: A golden plaque with Japanese text '兵庫県但馬牛 神戸肉' (Hyogo Prefecture Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef), displayed on a wooden stand, signifying the restaurant’s specialty in authentic Kobe Beef, reinforcing the narrator's description of the "world-renowned" A5 Wagyu.
